The scale of the reduction in Chinese oil imports is increasingly difficult to explain with available data. 

On paper, Beijing is currently running its economy with ~1/4 less oil than pre-war. But demand destruction (or shift in petchem feedstock) can hardly explain *all* of it. So is China drawing hard on difficult-to-track commercial stocks of refined products? Has it tapped underground SPR sites? All of the above?

quotedTweet.text:
China continues to cushion the global oil market, with imports May-to-date plunging significantly below even the depressed levels seen in April. 

At the current pace, Chinese crude oil imports are set to hit a 10-year low in May.  On a four-week average, Chinese crude/condesate imports are running >4 million b/d BELOW pre-war levels.

Price drives narrative. 

Narrative drives emotions. 

Most people are not forward looking. Physical oil traders are not that forward looking. They look at what they can lock in today and trade off of it. 

If you’ve been in the oil game long enough, you know there’s always an obvious setup. 

This is one of the those obvious moments. We’ve already reached the point of no return. No amount of jawboning will reverse the incoming inventory draws. 

We are going to witness the largest visible oil inventory draws in history over the coming weeks. 

Don’t let the emotions get the best of you. Focus on the data.
If there’s one thing you should be reading today, it’s this post.

text:
I don’t get how anyone arrives at the conclusion that there’s 5 million b/d of demand destruction. 

At the PEAK of COVID lockdowns, we lost ~20 million b/d. 

Think for a second here, are the mobility indices across the globe down 25% of the drop we saw in COVID?

Chaotic times make even the most rational person stop using their brain. Use it, that’s why you have one.

Look, it’s really simple. 

I find all this fake news jawboning amusing because if the oil math was off, they don’t need to do any of it. 

The more desperate they are, the more I am convinced that the math is right. 

It is what it is.
And the more they try and jawbone, the more the Iranians know how desperate the US is.

This is so funny. 

Now it’s verified as fake news.

I guess the consolation prize is that at least they admitted it was fake news?

🤷‍♂️
I thought it was obvious from the source. I guess not.

A closure of the Strait of Hormuz through August raises the risk of an economic downturn that comes close to the scale of the Great Recession in 2008, according to Rapidan Energy Group.

The advisory firm’s base case assumes the waterway reopens in July, resulting in an average oil demand reduction of 2.6 million barrels a day and the spot-market price for benchmark Brent crude peaking near $130 a barrel over the summer.

However, a disruption beyond then would require even greater demand erosion to offset the supply shock through August and September — potentially enough to trigger an annual decline in global oil consumption in 2026. Several leading forecasters already expect a rare contraction in worldwide demand this year.

In his article in the Iran Daily outlet, Gharibabadi contends the days of free and open shipping through the vital shipping channel were based on "comity and mutual good faith," but that the war launched by the U.S. and Israel on Feb. 28 had brought a "fundamental change of circumstances."

#USOIL 🇺🇸 #Stockpiles 🛢️⛽️

US oil inventories are collapsing:

Total US crude inventories, including the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR), fell by -17.8M 🛢️ last week, the largest weekly drawdown in history. https://t.co/dsxDZZI1aI
U.S. Crude Inventories Fall 9.1 Million Barrels, Nearly Three Times Larger Than Expected   – https://t.co/UjR0pw0jTT https://t.co/AoEoDCcNXs

The full EIA picture just dropped.

Total US crude inventories fell 17.8 million barrels last week. Commercial crude down 7.9 million. SPR down 9.9 million. The largest single-week SPR drain on record.

Since the Iran conflict began, 42 million barrels have been pulled from the US strategic reserve. That’s 10% of the entire SPR. Gone.
The SPR now stands at 374 million barrels. Lowest since July 2024.

This isn’t a bridge anymore. The emergency cushion is disappearing faster than at any point in history.
American production isn’t the backup plan. It’s the only plan left.

🚨 THE U.S. JUST DRAINED ITS EMERGENCY OIL RESERVE AT THE FASTEST PACE IN HISTORY.

🛢 The combined draw from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and commercial crude inventories hit 17.8 MILLION barrels last week — the largest weekly drawdown ever recorded.

⚠️ The SPR alone lost 9.9 MILLION barrels in a single week, breaking the previous all-time record set just one week earlier.

The reserve rebuilt from 347M to 415M barrels is now being drained rapidly as the Iran conflict pressures global energy markets.

📉 The SPR now holds roughly 374 MILLION barrels, far below its 727 MILLION peak in 2009.

The buffer built to protect the global economy from an oil crisis is now being consumed by the crisis itself.

#Oil #SPR #Energy #Markets #WTI

US manufacturing activity surged in May to its strongest level in four years as companies rushed to front-run the inflation shock triggered by the Middle East excursion — because nothing says “healthy economy” like panic-buying raw materials before the next oil tanker disappears from the Strait of Hormuz. 

Factory activity hit a 48-month high while input prices posted their biggest jump since 2022, supply chains slowed again, and businesses quietly started cutting jobs as costs exploded. In short, manufacturers are still producing, consumers are still paying more, and economists are once again discovering that “temporary inflation” has a remarkable talent for overstaying its welcome.

So far this year, we have signed and finalized oil tanker orders totaling more than 6 million DWT, and construction will start on 42 vessels within the year," said Peng Guisheng, director of the marketing department at CSSC Dalian Shipbuilding. #OilTanker #ChinaShipbuilding

It was just a normal day. I was rushing to work and decided to take a boda boda to save time, never imagining that I wouldn’t even make it to the office.

As we were heading to town, an overspeeding oil tanker hit us from behind and sped away without stopping. In a matter of seconds, everything changed. I was thrown onto the road, my phone was crushed, I sustained  injuries together with the rider, and the boda boda was completely damaged.

It was one of the most terrifying and painful moments of my life. One minute, I was focused on work and the responsibilities of the day; the next, I was lying on the road, fighting through shock and pain.

In that moment, I realized how fragile life truly is and how quickly everything can change without warning.

Despite the severity of the accident, I thank God for His protection and mercy. What happened could have ended very differently, but by His grace, I was given another chance at life.

I am deeply grateful to the good Samaritans who came to my rescue, offered help without hesitation, and ensured I reached the hospital in time to receive treatment. Your kindness during one of the darkest moments of my life will never be forgotten.

Recovery is ongoing, I am thankful to be alive and healing each day. This experience has reminded me never to take life, health, or the people around me for granted.

I left home expecting an ordinary day at work, but instead, I found myself facing one of the greatest tests of my life. Today, my heart is filled with gratitude because I survived.

Life is precious. Every new day is a gift, and every breath is a blessing.

Thank you, God, for protecting me and giving me another opportunity to live. 🙏❤️

Even with their vast wealth, MBZ and the UAE are overextended and must now consolidate and regroup. They left OPEC in an attempt to crack the global oil market, but the move has strained their finances. The treasury has had to extend them a lifeline. While they will likely stabilize in three or four years, no one is prepared to pour $50 billion — or even $5 billion — into Syria in the near term.Al-Sharaa is a transitional placeholder. He is not positioned to be part of Syria’s longer-term future; that is not his role. His job is to maintain stability for the time being, using as little money as possible while keeping the restless population calm.
